summary: Do not ship. P-2 remains mechanically unresolved: the combined-branch release approval cannot authorize the producer-only PR-A under the mandatory landing freshness gate. No other new critical issue was found within scope, and no simpler safe alternative exists under the confirmed single-flow constraint.

findings.title: P-2 remains unresolved: the combined approval cannot land PR-A
findings.body: The plan runs the sole release gate against the combined slice-1-plus-slice-2 HEAD, then asks `landing` to create PR-A containing only slice 1. Landing must run `--gate-check` on the current branch, while release freshness requires the approved `reviewedSha` to remain its ancestor and permits only later review bookkeeping. A producer-only PR-A must either use an earlier/sibling tip that excludes the reviewed SHA or remove slice 2 after approval; both fail closed. The normal PR path also publishes the current branch rather than selecting commits. Following this plan therefore requires bypassing the gate or makes PR-A impossible, eliminating the producer-first heartbeat boundary and collapsing P-2 back toward a single landing.
findings.recommendation: Attest each deployable branch shape separately: run verification and release approval for finalized PR-A, merge and verify the heartbeat, then construct PR-B on updated `main` and repeat verification/release. If one flow and one pre-landing release gate are mandatory, first extend and test the landing/status tooling with a checkpoint model that explicitly attests both exact PR tips.

next_steps: Revise the rollout contract so PR-A has a gate-valid landing transition, then rerun the focused P-2 review.
